titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University Department of Rural Sociology records21-33-1085
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Guy Smith

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-02-03 23:07:49 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Department of Rural Sociology records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: New York State College of Agriculture and Life Sciences. Department of Rural Sociology

did.abstract: Includes minutes of the New York State Interdepartmental Committee on Farm and Food Processing Labor (1948, 1950-1955); descriptions and reports concerning migrant labor in New York State and various funded projects; requests for information; and other records of Olaf F. Larson at the New York State College of Agriculture Department of Rural Sociology at Cornell University. Also, reports and bulletins (1920-1955), department newsletters (1961-1967), mimeographed proposals, notices, and position papers relating to challenges to governance at Cornell, collected by Harold Capener, 1965-1977 and Capener's correspondence (1963-1977).

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series II. Requests for Pamphlets and/or Information Box 1 Folders 3-8
Series III. New York State Interdepartmental Committee on Farm and Food Processing labor Box 1 Folders 9-16
Series IV. New York State Farm Labor Program, Procedural Manual Box 1 Folders17-30
Series V. Migrant Camps in New York Box 1 Folders 31-46
Series VI. Other Colleges, Campus-Wide Events, Rural Sociology Faculty and Students Box 1 Folders 47-61
Series VII. Correspondence of Harold R. Capener, 1963-1977 Boxes 1 Folders 62-69; Box 2 Folders 1-22
Series VIII. Department of Rural Sociology, Publications, Reports, Extension Reports (Bound Volumes and Various Folders)Boxes 2 Folder 23 - Box 4

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Department of Rural Sociology records, #21-33-1085. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The Cornell University Department of Rural Sociology records documents the functioning of the Department, 1920-2010. It includes minutes of the New York State Interdepartmental Committee on Farm and Food Processing Labor (1948, 1950-1955); descriptions and reports concerning migrant labor in New York State and various funded projects; requests for information; and other records of Olaf F. Larson at the New York State College of Agriculture Department of Rural Sociology at Cornell University. Also, reports and bulletins (1920-1955), department newsletters (1961-1967), mimeographed proposals, notices, and position papers relating to challenges to governance at Cornell, collected by Harold Capener (1965-1977), Capener's correspondence (1963-1977), and a bibliography: "One Act Plays for Mixed Casts" compiled by the Department of Rural Social Organization (mimeograph), post-1914.

controlaccess.subject: Migrant agricultural laborers.
Food industry and trade -- Employees.
Agricultural laborers.
controlaccess.corpname: New York (State). Interdepartmental Committee on Farm and Food Processing Labor

did.unittitle: Interview of Olaf Larson by Paul Eberts
Series II. Requests for Pamphlets and/or Information
Requests for pamphlets and/or information answering and making requests
Series III. New York State Interdepartmental Committee on Farm and Food Processing labor
New York State Interdepartmental Committee on Farm and Food Processing Labor Newsletters
Minutes, and Parts of Miscellaneous Drafts
Series IV. New York State Farm Labor Program, Procedural Manual
New York State Farm Labor Program, Procedural ManualIntroduction, TOC, Index
Section IX Ordering and Placing Workers
Series V. Migrant Camps in New York
Migrant camps in New York, carbon transcripts describing camps
Clinton, New York, Annual Reports, correspondence, newspaper clippings
Suffolk County, Migrant Labor Committee, Minutes of Meeting
Annual Report on Migrant Contract Systems, summaries, comments
Report of New York State Migrant Child Care Program by Dr. Howard E. Thomas
New York State migrants, carbon transcript on camp management, employer-labor relations. Copies of MIGRATOR, 1946 (mimeo newsletter), sample copies of records, etc.
Farm labor letters (mimeo letters to county agriculture agents from Cooperative Extension in Agriculture and Home Economics)
Home Economics Projects in Migrant Camps Case Study
Investigation Report on Migrant Labor Camps
Series VI. Other Colleges, Campus-Wide Events, Rural Sociology Faculty and Students
Other colleges, campus-wide events, Rural Sociology faculty and students resolution, College of Architecture, Art and Planning
Staff Organization Constitution, School of Industrial and Labor Relations
Report of Survey of Cadet Alumni of the Cornell Army, Navy and Air Force ROTC by Lillian Wilson
Letter to department heads and chairman, College of Agriculture
Letter to all university units from Committee on Mass Media
Proposed Study of New York State Farm Labor System
Letter to faculty, research proposal from students of Rural Sociology
Department of Rural Sociology, "Major Social Problems in the United States," "Social Problems Listed by Rural Sociology Faculty."
"A Resolution from FCSA for Adoption by the University Faculty," "Faculty Statement" (with marginal notes), Memos Concerning Secretarial, Clerical Staff and Restructuring Committee
Series VII. Correspondence of Harold R. Capener, 1963-1977
Series VIII. Department of Rural Sociology, Publications, Reports, Extension Reports (Bound Volumes and Various Folders)
Department of Rural Sociology, publications, reports, extension reports (bound volumes and various folders)
"The Staff and Program of the Department of Rural Sociology"
Department of Rural Sociology academic staff
Christmas letter, change of address form
"Rural Sociology in the Nineties" - CSRS Review (includes faculty vitae)

"Comprehensive Review of the Department of Rural Sociology" Sponsored by: USDA Cooperative State Research, Education and Extension Service, Cornell University Faculty Committee on Program (includes faculty vitae) Reviews
Department of Rural Sociology Annual Reports of Extension Work and Special Reports
Department of Rural Sociology Annual Extension Reports 1943-45
Department of Rural Sociology Annual Extension Reports
Department of Rural Sociology Annual Extension Reports 1950-1955
Rural Sociology. extension mimeographs I - Recreation
Rural Sociology. extension mimeographs III - Rural Community
Papers for fiftieth anniversary of Rural Sociology at Cornell, 1915-1965
Dept. of Rural Sociology and Graduate Field of Development Sociology-Publication
New York State Extension Farm Labor Program, 1947; State Farm Labor Staff, 1944-1945; Tour of the Farm Labor Projects for Cornell, 1945
Migrant Camp Personnel-Stout, Bullard, Floyd, Neeley,1947; Bernice Scott, 1982
Home Demonstration Agents-Chemung County Survey, 1946; Agaton Pol and Ben Chitamber , 1958; Dr. Ernest Neal, 1958; Earl Regiman and Family, 1948; Rural Sociology Seminar Room in Mann, 1952
Group of Graduate Students and Faculty who Organize Work Abroad- Polson, Deening, Winsor
